channel.title: Posts on Aletta Zhao
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Aletta Zhao

item.title: Time Is a Snake Biting Its Own Tail
item.description: Márquez lays out a peculiar notion of time in the rise and fall of the Buendía family. Not linear, not cyclical, but something closer to a Möbius strip.

item.title: In Defence of Useless Knowledge
item.description: Whenever someone asks what my studies are good for, I think of that tree in Zhuangzi — the one that survived precisely because it was useless to the carpenter.

item.title: A Winter Afternoon in the Café
item.description: The sky outside is the colour of lead. The heating has fogged the window. Someone at the next table draws a sun on the glass with a fingertip.
